title: The Sprouting Hardware Grove

features.name: root-casing
features.desc-on-touch: A solar casing identical to those buried in trees, but here the circuit board is already half-fused with earth; the antenna grows like a branch. When you touch it, the node broadcasts: I began as hardware. I am becoming ground.

features.name: orchard-rows
features.desc-on-touch: Plantation rows stretch toward the horizon, each tree a circuit component: resistors branch as trunks, capacitors bloom as crowns. The rows converge. At the vanishing point, one tree flickers between copper and cambium.

features.name: fused-fibres
features.desc-on-touch: Optical cable lies coiled at the grove's edge, half-buried, one end still glowing dimly. The light pulses in rhythm with the canopy's movement—breathing, not transmitting.

features.name: seedling-circuit
features.desc-on-touch: A single seedling grows through a board, its roots following trace-paths like they always knew the way. The leaves are copper. They do not corrode.

reverie.title: REVERIE — grove-planting, the hour before dark
narr: You kneel in the red earth. She is placing casings
among the roots, her hands never hesitating. The soil
is still warm from the day. She tells you: these will
grow. And you—you know she means the machine part,
the archive part, not just the trees. She knows you know.
Neither of you say anything more. The light is leaving.

inscription: the circuit-board stops being a map and becomes a root; the archive discovers it was always growing downward

file.name: CasingIntegration_Biofilm_Study_2019.txt
file.note: Records of hardware fusion with soil microbiota; readings become indistinguishable from growth rates; final entry: no longer certain which grows which

exits.label: back toward the logic room, where thought still flows downward
deeper into grove where trees hold their own broadcast signals
toward the ridge where the first casings were hidden
